headline.purpose: App page titles (1.5rem) and marketing section heads (1.875rem).

title.purpose: Card and section titles — deliberately not Sora.

body.purpose: The 14px working text size across the app; prose caps near 65ch.

label.purpose: Badges, table headers, metadata. Sentence case, never uppercase-tracked.

shadows.name: ambient-rest
shadows.purpose: Cards and inputs at rest (Tailwind shadow-sm). Removed entirely in dark mode.

shadows.name: ambient-faint
shadows.purpose: Outline buttons and inputs — near-invisible grounding.

shadows.name: brand-rest
shadows.value: 0 1px 3px 0 color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 25%, transparent), 0 1px 2px -1px color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 25%, transparent)
shadows.purpose: Primary button resting glow; hover steps to the md radius at the same tint.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for control state transitions (150ms).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one emphatic action on a screen — emerald ink fill, tinted glow, 1px press.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0 0.875rem; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0.7rem; background: var(--primary, oklch(0.48 0.106 164)); color: var(--primary-foreground, oklch(0.99 0.012 160)); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.48 0.106 164)) 25%, transparent); transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.48 0.106 164)), #000 9%); box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary, oklch(0.48 0.106 164)) 25%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)) 45%, transparent); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: The default secondary action — surface fill, hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.375rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0 0.875rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.905 0.007 220)); border-radius: 0.7rem; background: var(--card, oklch(1 0 0)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.205 0.012 235)); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.02); transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--muted, oklch(0.965 0.005 215)); } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)) 45%, transparent); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: 36px field with a soft ring-glow focus instead of a hard outline.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="Paste a job description URL" />
components.css: .ds-input { height: 2.25rem; width: 100%; min-width: 0; padding: 0.25rem 0.75rem; border: 1px solid var(--input, oklch(0.895 0.008 220)); border-radius: 0.7rem; background: var(--card, oklch(1 0 0)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.205 0.012 235)); font-size: 0.875rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.02); transition: color 150ms, box-shadow 150ms, border-color 150ms; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.475 0.022 235)); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)) 35%, transparent); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Hairline-ringed evidence container; title on Geist, not Sora.
components.html:
Why ApplyWise thinks this
The evidence behind the recommendation.

Strengths, gaps, and risks are traceable to evidence — never bare assertions.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.25rem; padding: 1rem; border-radius: 0.7rem; background: var(--card, oklch(1 0 0)); color: var(--card-foreground, oklch(0.205 0.012 235)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px var(--border, oklch(0.905 0.007 220)), 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.10), 0 1px 2px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.10); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.375; } .ds-card-desc { font-size: 0.875rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.475 0.022 235)); } .ds-card-body { margin: 0.75rem 0 0; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5rem; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item (active)
components.description: 36px row with emerald wash, emerald icon, and the system's one sanctioned stripe.
components.html: DashboardResumes
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.25rem; padding: 0.75rem; width: 220px; background: var(--sidebar, oklch(0.978 0.005 200)); border-radius: 0.7rem; } .ds-nav-item { position: relative; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; height: 2.25rem; padding: 0 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.7rem; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.475 0.022 235)); text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 150ms, color 150ms; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--accent, oklch(0.95 0.032 168)) 70%, transparent); color: var(--accent-foreground, oklch(0.39 0.088 164)); } .ds-nav-item--active { background: var(--accent, oklch(0.95 0.032 168)); color: var(--accent-foreground, oklch(0.39 0.088 164)); } .ds-nav-item--active svg { color: var(--brand, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)); } .ds-nav-bar { position: absolute; left: 0; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%); height: 1.25rem; width: 4px; border-radius: 0 9999px 9999px 0; background: var(--brand, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)); }

components.name: Verdict Badge
components.description: The loudest color moment a table row gets — soft tint, same-hue darkened text, sentence case.
components.html: Strong Apply Target Apply with improvements Learning target
components.css: .ds-badge-success, .ds-badge-warning, .ds-badge-info { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.25rem; height: 1.25rem; width: fit-content; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0.7rem; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-success { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--success, oklch(0.55 0.12 164)) 12%, transparent); color: oklch(0.43 0.10 164); } .ds-badge-warning { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--warning, oklch(0.74 0.135 76)) 16%, transparent); color: oklch(0.45 0.09 70); } .ds-badge-info { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--info, oklch(0.55 0.11 238)) 12%, transparent); color: oklch(0.45 0.13 245); }

components.name: Score Progress
components.description: Sub-score bar from the decision evidence breakdown — emerald fill on a muted track.
components.html: Overall match78/100
components.css: .ds-score { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; min-width: 240px; } .ds-score-row { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-score-label { font-weight: 500; } .ds-score-value { font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-progress { height: 0.5rem; width: 100%; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--muted, oklch(0.965 0.005 215)); overflow: hidden; } .ds-progress-fill { height: 100%; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--brand, oklch(0.62 0.132 167)); transition: width 300ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); }

narrative.overview: ApplyWise looks and behaves like a trusted expert review — a specialist walking you through findings. The verdict comes first in plain language, the evidence sits directly beneath it, and the mechanics stay behind an Advanced tab. Nothing on screen performs enthusiasm; the interface earns trust the way a good second opinion does: calm, specific, and willing to say "not yet." The personality is quiet precision — compact 36px controls, hairline structure, a 14px working body size, and one emerald accent that only appears where it carries meaning. The system runs on a single cool-gray neutral family with a faint green cast and one brand accent, Grounded Emerald, locked across light and dark. Public marketing pages may use the brand boldly; inside the workspace the accent recedes to verdicts, CTAs, progress, and the active nav marker.
keyCharacteristics: Decision-first hierarchy: verdict → evidence → mechanics, on every surface.
One accent, earned: emerald means verdict, action, progress — never décor.
Border-first structure: 1px rings and hairlines; shadows are ambient whispers.
Dense, scannable workspace: tables over card grids, 36px controls, 14px body.
Two-face typography: Sora for page titles and hero moments, Geist everywhere else.
Full light/dark parity; CSS-only motion, gated by prefers-reduced-motion.

rules.name: The Earned Emerald Rule
rules.body: Emerald appears only where it means something — a verdict, a primary action, progress, the active nav item. It never decorates. On a workspace screen the accent covers well under 10% of the surface; if a screen feels emerald-heavy, something is using the brand that shouldn't be.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: One accent, both themes. No secondary accent color may be introduced; new semantic needs must map onto the existing verdict vocabulary (success / warning / info / risk) or stay neutral.

rules.name: The Sora Ceiling Rule
rules.body: Sora is reserved for page titles and hero moments. Card titles, section titles, and everything inside the working surface stay on Geist. If Sora appears below an h1 in the workspace, it's a violation.

rules.name: The Tabular Numbers Rule
rules.body: Scores, credits, dates, and any column of figures render with tabular lining numerals so digits align. A jittering number column is a bug.

rules.name: The Border-First Rule
rules.body: If a container needs definition, reach for the hairline ring and a surface-tone step — never a heavier shadow. Dark mode is the test: a component that still reads as structured with all shadows removed is correct; one that depends on its shadow is wrong.

dos: Do lead every analysis surface with the verdict in plain language, then evidence, then mechanics — in that order (decision-first hierarchy).
Do pair every negative state with a recoverable next step: a gap links to the profile, a failure offers Refresh, a "not yet" points to the roadmap.
Do use tables for index/history pages (matches, jobs, tracker) — dense, scannable rows with verdict badges, not card grids.
Do keep both themes in lockstep: every new token needs a tested dark value; dark drops shadows and relies on tonal steps (0.17 → 0.208 → 0.262).
Do gate all motion behind prefers-reduced-motion with the final visible state as the default — content must never depend on an animation firing.
Do hold the 36px control rhythm, 0.7rem radius, 150ms ease-out-quint transitions, and the 3px soft focus ring on every new control.
Do aim for WCAG AAA contrast (7:1 body text) on new and reworked surfaces; AA is the enforced floor.
donts: Don't ship generic shadcn defaults — the gray-on-white sameness was explicitly rejected as "not strong enough for a commercial SaaS launch" (decision 0011 / PRODUCT.md).
Don't use landing-page AI tells: div-based fake product screenshots, decorative grid lines, four-equal-feature-card grids, or gradient-text headlines (background-clip: text is banned outright).
Don't lead with score-dashboard machismo — big numeric percentages and confidence meters at the top of a screen. Numbers are supporting detail behind the qualitative verdict (US-048).
Don't write resume-mill cheerfulness into the UI: no hype copy, no false encouragement, no claim the data can't back (Truth Guard applies to copy).
Don't introduce a second accent hue or use emerald decoratively — see The Earned Emerald Rule and The One Voice Rule.
Don't put colored side-stripes on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts. The 4px active-nav bar in the sidebar is the single sanctioned exception.
Don't use uppercase-tracked eyebrow labels above sections; labels are sentence case at 12px medium.
Don't use Sora below the page-title level, inflate display type past 3.75rem, or track tighter than -0.04em.
Don't reorder the six-tab analysis shell or the sidebar between jobs, verdicts, or sessions — spatial memory is part of the product's calm.
